definitions: Making a hole in something with a tool or by digging.
To make (a hole) in something with a tool or by digging.
To make (a tunnel or well) by digging or drilling.
To cause to feel weary and uninterested by being dull or tedious.
examples: The carpenter was boring a hole for the screw.
The insects were boring into the wood.
The machine is boring a tunnel through the mountain.
His long speech was boring the audience.
